title: Protein expression in Komagataella phaffii
author: Maja Rennig
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bd32i8qe
materials: Autoclave for 00:20:00 on liquid a cycle program

Grow the cells at 28 °C and 250 RPM of shaking It is crucial that the temperature does not exceed 28 °C when producing protein. It is advised to double-check the incubator's temperature reading with a thermometer.

steps.title: Prepare BMGY and BMMY media Ingredients...
steps.instruction: Prepare BMGY and BMMY media Ingredients (final concentrations): 1 Mass / % volume yeast extract 2 Mass / % volume peptone 100 millimolar (mM) mM potassium phosphate, pH 6.0 1.34 Mass / % volume YNB 0.0004 Mass / % volume biotin 1 % volume glycerol (BMGY) or 0.5 % volume methanol (BMMY) K. phaffii cells exhibit optimal growth with higher YNB concentrations than S. cerevisiae

steps.title: Dissolve 10 g of yeast extract,...
steps.instruction: Dissolve 10 g of yeast extract, 20 g of peptone in 700 mL of water

steps.title: Let cool down to room temperature, then add the following
steps.instruction: Let cool down to room temperature, then add the following: 100 mL 1 Molarity (M) potassium phosphate buffer, pH 6 (autoclaved stock solution) 100 mL 13.4 Mass / % volume YNB with ammonium sulfate without amino acids (filter sterilized stock solution) 2 mL 0.02 Mass / % volume biotin (filter sterilized stock solution)

steps.title: For BMGY add 100 mL 10...
steps.instruction: For BMGY add 100 mL 10 % volume glycerol For BMMY add 100 mL 5 % volume methanol If not used directly, store the media at 4 °C for up to 2 months

steps.title: Prepare 1 Molarity (M) potassium phosphate buffer, pH 6
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 Molarity (M) potassium phosphate buffer, pH 6

steps.title: Transfer 2
steps.instruction: Transfer 2.5 mL of the saturated culture into 100 mL BMGY in a 500 mL shake flask with low baffles and grow overnight at 28 °C It is also possible to grow the cells at 30 °C

steps.title: Measure OD 600 of the overnight culture
steps.instruction: Measure OD 600 of the overnight culture

steps.title: Pellet 50 mL of the culture...
steps.instruction: Pellet 50 mL of the culture in a 50 mL falcon tube at 4000 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes

steps.title: Inoculate 25 mL of BMGY in...
steps.instruction: Inoculate 25 mL of BMGY in a 250 mL shake flask with a single colony of K. phaffii GS115 with the desired construct integrated on the genome.

steps.title: Prepare 13
steps.instruction: Prepare 13.4 Mass / % volume YNB with ammonium sulfate without amino acids

steps.title: Prepare 0
steps.instruction: Prepare 0.2 Mass / % volume biotin solution

steps.title: Combine 132 mL of 1 Molarity...
steps.instruction: Combine 132 mL of 1 Molarity (M) K 2 HPO 4 and 868 mL of 1 Molarity (M) KH 2 PO 4

steps.title: Confirm that the pH 6
steps.instruction: Confirm that the pH 6.0 and adjust using phosphoric acid or KOH

steps.title: Sterilize by autoclaving for 00:20:00 on...
steps.instruction: Sterilize by autoclaving for 00:20:00 on liquid cycle program and store at Room temperature The shelf life of this solution is greater than one year

steps.title: Dissolve 134 g of yeast nitrogen...
steps.instruction: Dissolve 134 g of yeast nitrogen base (YNB) with ammonium sulfate and without amino acids in 1.000 L of water. The solution can be heated to dissolve YNB completely in water Alternatively, use 34 g of YNB without ammonium sulfate and amino acids and 100 g of ammonium sulfate.

steps.title: Sterilize by filteration and store at...
steps.instruction: Sterilize by filteration and store at 4 °C The shelf life of this solution is approximately one year

steps.title: Dissolve 20 mg biotin in 100 mL of water
steps.instruction: Dissolve 20 mg biotin in 100 mL of water

steps.title: Sterilize by filteration and store at...
steps.instruction: Sterilize by filteration and store at 4 °C The shelf life of this solution is approximately one year

steps.title: Incubate the culture at 28 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the culture at 28 °C with 250RPM shaking until saturation It is also possible to grow the cells at 30 °C

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in BMMY medium to a final OD 600 of 50. The appropriate amount of media can be calculated as: V_{BMMY} = \frac{OD_{600,measured}\cdot V_{pre-culture}}{50}

steps.title: Add 1% methanol to the expression...
steps.instruction: Add 1% methanol to the expression culture ( 1 mL of 100 % volume methanol to 100 mL of BMMY culture) around 24 hours after inoculation

steps.title: Add 1% methanol to the expression...
steps.instruction: Add 1% methanol to the expression culture ( 1 mL of 100 % volume methanol to 100 mL of BMMY culture) around 24 hours after last addition of methanol

steps.title: Add 1% methanol to the expression...
steps.instruction: Add 1% methanol to the expression culture ( 1 mL of 100 % volume methanol to 100 mL of BMMY culture) around 24 hours after last addition of methanol

steps.title: Harvest the cells by centrifugation at...
steps.instruction: Harvest the cells by centrifugation at 4000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and discard the pellets

steps.title: Keep the sample On ice when...
steps.instruction: Keep the sample On ice when working with it and at 4 °C or -20 °C for storage

steps.title: Inoculate 100 mL BMMY medium with...
steps.instruction: Inoculate 100 mL BMMY medium with 2 mL of the normalized pre-culture, giving a start OD 600 of 1.
